title: Design a DAG from dependencies and grain
description: Lay out tasks by their true data dependencies with idempotent, date-parameterized units before wiring schedules.
body: Use when building a new pipeline DAG. Do not chain tasks by convenient ordering — encode the real data dependencies so the scheduler can parallelize and recover correctly. Actions: identify each task's inputs and outputs, draw edges only for true dependencies, make each task idempotent and parameterized by execution date, and set fan-out where independent work can run in parallel. Validation signals include a DAG whose edges match real data dependencies and tasks that rerun cleanly for a given date. Risk boundary: false or missing dependencies cause races or wasted serialization; if two tasks share hidden state not expressed as an edge, treat it as a correctness risk and escalate to model the dependency explicitly.
